intro: Rumicastrum arenicola, also known as Sand Rumic, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its yellow flowers and fleshy leaves. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Rumicastrum arenicola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote digestion and relieve skin irritations.
